info.name: Star Wars Movies API
info.description:
Collection for the Star Wars Movies API deployed on Railway. Includes auth, user, and movie endpoints with JWT-based access control.

Base URL: https://starwars-movies-api.up.railway.app

To authenticate:
1. Run **POST /auth/login** (or /auth/register).
2. Copy the `access_token` from the response.
3. Open the collection → Variables → set the `token` value.
4. All protected requests will use `Bearer {{token}}` automatically.

variable.description: JWT access token. Paste the value of access_token from /auth/login or /auth/register.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Public authentication endpoints. No token required.

request.description:
Registers a new user with role USER (default).

**Public** — no token required.

Returns `access_token` and the created user object (password excluded).

**Validations:**
- email: must be a valid email
- username: minimum 3 characters
- password: minimum 6 characters

Authenticates an existing user and returns a JWT access token.

**Public** — no token required.

Copy the `access_token` value from the response and paste it into the collection variable `token` to authenticate subsequent requests.

Registers a new user with role ADMIN.

**Public** — no token required.

Requires `adminSecret` matching the server's `ADMIN_SECRET` environment variable. Returns 403 if the secret is wrong.

Returns `access_token` and the created admin user object.

Returns the profile of the currently authenticated user (id, email, username, role, timestamps). Password is excluded from the response.

**Requires:** Bearer token (USER or ADMIN role).

item.name: Movies (Public)
item.description: Movie listing and detail endpoints. GET /movies is public; GET /movies/:id requires authentication.

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of all movies.

**Public** — no token required.

Default: page=1, limit=10.

Response shape: `{ data: { data: Movie[], total, page, limit }, statusCode, timestamp }`

Filters movies by partial title match (case-insensitive, uses ILIKE internally).

**Public** — no token required.

Example: `?title=hope` returns all movies whose title contains "hope".

Filters movies by partial director name match (case-insensitive).

**Public** — no token required.

Example: `?director=lucas` returns movies directed by George Lucas.

Filters movies by exact episode ID.

**Public** — no token required.

Example: `?episode=4` returns only Episode IV — A New Hope.

Returns the first 3 movies (page 1, limit 3).

**Public** — no token required.

Combine with `title`, `director`, or `episode` filters as needed.

variable.description: UUID of the movie to retrieve

request.description:
Returns the full detail of a single movie by its UUID.

**Requires:** Bearer token (USER or ADMIN role).

Returns 404 if the movie does not exist.

item.name: Movies (Admin)
item.description: Movie management endpoints. All require an ADMIN JWT token.

body.raw:
{
  "title": "A New Hope",
  "episodeId": 4,
  "director": "George Lucas",
  "producer": "Gary Kurtz",
  "releaseDate": "1977-05-25",
  "openingCrawl": "It is a period of civil war..."
}

request.description:
Creates a new movie record.

**Requires:** Bearer token with ADMIN role.

Only `title` is required; all other fields are optional. Returns 409 if a movie with the same title already exists.

variable.description: UUID of the movie to update

request.description:
Partially updates an existing movie. Only the provided fields are modified.

**Requires:** Bearer token with ADMIN role.

Returns 404 if the movie does not exist. Returns 409 if the updated title conflicts with an existing movie.

variable.description: UUID of the movie to delete

request.description:
Deletes a movie by its UUID.

**Requires:** Bearer token with ADMIN role.

Returns **204 No Content** on success (empty body). Returns 404 if the movie does not exist.

Fetches all Star Wars films from SWAPI (`https://www.swapi.tech/api/films`) and upserts them into the database.

**Requires:** Bearer token with ADMIN role.

This is the same operation executed automatically by the daily cron job (runs at 1:00 AM). Safe to call multiple times — existing movies are updated, not duplicated.

Returns: `{ synced: number, message: string }`
